claim: Loading local projects into Bolt.new-like tools works well but has practical limits around 100 files to avoid site crashes during import
actionable: When importing projects to web-based AI coding tools, keep imports under ~100 files and avoid node_modules to prevent frontend memory/parsing issues
source_title: Our Bolt.new Fork's Most Requested Feature is FINALLY Here

claim: Reverting to specific messages in agent conversations allows scrapping work from the last message or set of messages without losing entire conversation history
actionable: Implement conversation reversion UI in agent tools to allow users to undo failed agent outputs while preserving context history
source_title: Our Bolt.new Fork's Most Requested Feature is FINALLY Here

claim: When importing projects, users often need to explicitly tell agents to run installation and execution commands (npm install, npm run dev) rather than agents assuming these automatically
actionable: After importing a project to agent tools, explicitly prompt the agent with setup instructions including dependency installation and dev server startup
source_title: Our Bolt.new Fork's Most Requested Feature is FINALLY Here

claim: Forking a chat from a specific message is a critical feature for exploratory agent work, allowing parallel exploration without losing earlier progress
actionable: Implement chat forking UI at message level in agent tools to enable branching exploration of multiple solution paths
source_title: Our Bolt.new Fork's Most Requested Feature is FINALLY Here

claim: Prompt caching for agent coding tools reduces redundant processing when the same system prompts and context are reused across multiple agent calls
actionable: Enable prompt caching in your agent setup to reduce token spend when using the same system prompt and tool definitions across multiple agent invocations
source_title: Our Bolt.new Fork's Most Requested Feature is FINALLY Here
